title: selliguea wrightii
intro: Selliguea wrightii, also known as Wright's polypody or Wright's spleenwort, is a type of fern native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, arthritis, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Selliguea wrightii include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tea, with the dried leaves and stems used to make infusions or capsules for internal use.
